steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence mentions 'two other Black Americans who served in Congress' and then names them. The punctuation must introduce that list of specific names.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Congress.: Splits the sentence after a category but leaves the names that follow as a fragment with no verb. ✗
  • Choice B — Congress;: Joins two independent clauses; the names alone are not an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice C — Congress: Leaves the connection between the general category and the specific names grammatically incomplete and awkward. ✗
  • Choice D — Congress:: Introduces a list or elaboration that follows — exactly what's needed to move from the general group to the two specific names. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
